title: Hymn Of Trust
content:
O Love Divine, that stooped to share
    Our sharpest pang, our bitterest tear,
    On Thee we cast each earth-born care,
    We smile at pain while Thou art near!

    Though long the weary way we tread,
    And sorrow crown each lingering year,
    No path we shun, no darkness dread,
    Our hearts still whispering, Thou art near!

    When drooping pleasure turns to grief,
    And trembling faith is changed to fear,
    The murmuring wind, the quivering leaf,
    Shall softly tell us, Thou art near!

    On Thee we fling our burdening woe,
    O Love Divine, forever dear,
    Content to suffer while we know,
    Living and dying, Thou art near!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Oliver Wendell Holmes, titled "Hymn Of Trust", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
